evals.prompt: I want to build a personal CLI tool that watches a folder and auto-organizes downloaded files by type and date. Set it up so Claude Code can build it autonomously, end to end.
evals.expected_output: A drop-in .claude/ folder with a tailored CLAUDE.md + DESIGN.md (keystone = the rules/classification model), pre-seeded GOAL/STATE/PROGRESS/DECISIONS, /start /goal /loop /status commands, spine agents + project-specific component-owner agents, hooks, scripts, githooks. No leftover {{placeholders}}.

evals.prompt: Recreate the same autonomous build blueprint for a new idea: a Discord bot that summarizes long threads on demand. Make it a drop-in .claude folder.
evals.expected_output: A tailored .claude/ harness for the bot: keystone identified (e.g., the message/thread model), wrap-vs-build decision recorded (wrap the Discord lib), component-owner subagents derived (e.g., gateway-engineer, summarizer-engineer), build order in DESIGN.md, everything filled in.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an autonomous Claude Code setup for a Rust HTTP load-testing tool I'm starting from scratch.
evals.expected_output: A .claude/ harness with Rust set as the hot-path language in DECISIONS (D2), keystone = the request/plan model, component-owners like scheduler-engineer / worker-engineer / reporting-engineer, spine agents, and the full drop-in folder ready for /start.
